solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String strWithout3a3b(int a, int b) {
        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
        while (a > 0 || b > 0) {
            if (a > b) {
                if (a >= 2) {
                    sb.append("aa");
                    a -= 2;
                } else {
                    sb.append('a');
                    a--;
                }
                if (b > 0) {
                    sb.append('b');
                    b--;
                }
            } else {
                if (b >= 2) {
                    sb.append("bb");
                    b -= 2;
                } else {
                    sb.append('b');
                    b--;
                }
                if (a > 0) {
                    sb.append('a');
                    a--;
                }
            }
        }
        return sb.toString();
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(A + B)
space complexity: O(A + B)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The algorithm greedily constructs the string by appending two of the more frequent character and one of the less frequent character in each iteration, ensuring no three consecutive characters. This avoids the substrings 'aaa' and 'bbb'. The approach is efficient and works within the problem constraints.>
